title: Wren and the Big Puddle Day
synopsis: When it rains all day, Wren thinks her playdate is ruined. But with help from her friends Maya and Kai, she discovers that the biggest puddles make the biggest splashes and the most fun memories.

pages.text: Wren pressed her nose against the window. Rain drops raced down the glass like tiny rivers. "Oh no!" she said. "Maya and Kai are coming over to play. But it's so wet outside!"

illustration.description: A young Black girl with curly hair in colorful hair ties looking out a rain-streaked window with a disappointed expression. Her breath fogs the glass slightly. The room behind her is warm and cozy with bright yellows and oranges, while outside is gray and rainy.
illustration.alt: Wren looking sadly out the window at the rain

pages.text: Mom came into the room carrying a tray of juice boxes. "What's wrong, little bird?" she asked. Mom always called Wren 'little bird' because of her name. "We planned to play in the yard," Wren sighed. "Now everything is ruined!"

illustration.description: A tall Black woman with short natural hair wearing a bright purple sweater, holding a tray with colorful juice boxes. She has a kind, understanding expression as she looks at Wren. The room has warm lighting with family photos on the walls.
illustration.alt: Wren's mom holding juice boxes and talking to Wren

pages.text: Just then, the doorbell rang. DING DONG! "That must be your friends," Mom said with a smile. Wren ran to the door. Maya stood there with her bright red rain boots. Kai had his blue umbrella. "Ready to play?" Maya asked with a big grin.

illustration.description: Two children at the front door - Maya, a Latina girl with long braided hair wearing bright red rain boots and a yellow raincoat, and Kai, an Asian boy with a cheerful smile holding a blue umbrella and wearing a green jacket. Both look excited despite the rain behind them.
illustration.alt: Maya and Kai at the door in their rain gear

pages.text: "But we can't play outside," Wren said sadly. "It's too wet and muddy." Kai tilted his head. "Who says we can't play in the rain?" Maya jumped up and down. "I LOVE playing in puddles! Don't you have rain boots, Wren?"

illustration.description: The three children in the doorway - Wren looking uncertain while Maya bounces excitedly and Kai has a thoughtful, encouraging expression. Maya's red boots are prominent and Kai's blue umbrella drips with raindrops. The mood is shifting from sad to hopeful.
illustration.alt: The three friends talking about playing in the rain

pages.text: Mom appeared with Wren's yellow rain boots. "These might help," she said with a wink. Wren looked at her boots, then at her friends, then at the biggest puddle she had ever seen in her backyard. It was like a tiny lake! "Maybe we could try," she said slowly.

illustration.description: Mom holding bright yellow rain boots while Wren considers them. Through the open door, a huge puddle is visible in the backyard, reflecting the gray sky like a mirror. Maya and Kai peer excitedly at the massive puddle, their eyes wide with wonder.
illustration.alt: Mom showing Wren her rain boots with the big puddle visible outside

pages.text: The three friends put on all their rain gear. Wren felt funny in her yellow boots. They were so big and made squishy sounds when she walked. SQUISH, SQUASH, SQUISH! Maya and Kai giggled. Soon Wren was giggling too.

illustration.description: All three children fully dressed in colorful rain gear - Wren in yellow boots and a bright orange raincoat, Maya in her red boots and yellow coat, Kai with his blue umbrella and green jacket. They're all laughing and making exaggerated walking motions, with motion lines showing the squishy boot sounds.
illustration.alt: The three friends in rain gear making squishy sounds with their boots

pages.text: They stepped outside into the rain. The first raindrop that hit Wren's nose made her laugh. "It tickles!" she said. Maya ran to the giant puddle first. "Watch this!" she shouted. SPLASH! Water flew everywhere like a fountain.

illustration.description: Maya mid-jump into the enormous puddle, creating a spectacular splash with water droplets flying in all directions like sparkling diamonds. Wren and Kai watch with amazement and joy from nearby, raindrops visible in the air around them. The scene is dynamic and full of motion.
illustration.alt: Maya making a big splash in the giant puddle

pages.text: "My turn!" Kai called. He jumped in with both feet. SPLASH-SPLOSH! Even bigger waves! Wren watched her friends having so much fun. She took a deep breath. "Here I go!" she yelled. She ran and jumped as high as she could.

illustration.description: Wren flying through the air with arms spread wide and a huge smile, about to land in the puddle. Maya and Kai are cheering her on with raised arms. The background shows their backyard with rain falling, and Wren's expression is one of pure joy and freedom.
illustration.alt: Wren jumping high into the air about to splash into the puddle

pages.text: MEGA-SPLASH! Wren made the biggest splash of all! Water went everywhere - on the fence, on the flowers, and all over her friends! They all laughed and laughed. "This is the BEST rainy day ever!" Wren shouted. And she meant every word.

illustration.description: All three children completely soaked but absolutely delighted, standing in and around the puddle with arms raised in celebration. Water droplets sparkle around them in the air, and their expressions show pure happiness. The gray rainy day now looks magical and fun rather than dreary.
illustration.alt: All three friends celebrating and laughing, completely wet but happy

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: Why was Wren upset at the beginning of the story?
choices: It was raining and she thought it would ruin playtime
questions.explanation: Wren was sad because it was raining and she thought her friends wouldn't be able to play outside in the yard as planned.

questions.question: What color were Wren's rain boots?
questions.explanation: Wren wore bright yellow rain boots that made squishy sounds when she walked.

questions.question: Who jumped in the puddle first?
questions.explanation: Maya ran to the giant puddle first and made the first big splash.

questions.question: How did Wren feel about the rainy day at the end?
choices: She thought it was the best rainy day ever
questions.explanation: By the end, Wren shouted that it was the BEST rainy day ever because she had so much fun with her friends.

questions.question: What sound did Wren's boots make?
questions.explanation: Wren's big yellow boots made squishy sounds - squish, squash, squish - when she walked.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words!
pages.text: Wren looked out the window at the ___1___. Her friends Maya and Kai came over wearing their ___2___ boots. At first Wren was ___3___, but then they all had fun jumping in the big ___4___!

pages.title: More Missing Words!
pages.text: Maya wore ___1___ boots and Kai had a ___2___ umbrella. Wren's boots made a ___3___ sound. When they jumped in the water, it went ___4___ everywhere!
